title: Bugged Queue: Incorrect Dequeue from Front and Rear

statement.markdown: A Queue has been implemented as a Doubly Linked List. Your task is to fix the dequeue operation at both the front and rear ends of the queue. The buggy implementation provided removes the element at the wrong end.
statement.inputFormat:
The input will be in the following format:
size of elements
x1 x2 x3 x4 ... xn
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the dequeued elements in the correct order and any output in the last line if applicable.
statement.notes: The input will not contain leading zeros.

constraints.description: Size of elements in the input
Size of elements in the input

examples.explanation: The elements in the order of dequeue.

hints.text: You should correctly implement the dequeue operation to remove the front and rear elements.

editorial.title: Bugged Queue: Incorrect Dequeue from Front and Rear
editorial.intuition: To dequeue an element, we need to check at the front and rear. If the queue is not empty at the front, then remove the front element. Otherwise, remove the element at the rear. It is important to maintain the head and tail pointers correctly.
editorial.bruteForce: Remove the node at the front end, update head as required, and repeat for the rear end.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by checking whether the head and tail pointers are updated correctly.
editorial.dryRun: Check if the head pointer points to the correct node after removing the front element. Repeat for the rear end.

buggyStarterCode.python:
def poll(self):
  if self.head is None:
    print("poll: queue is empty")
    return
  if self.head.next is None:
    print("poll: " + str(self.head.data))
    node = self.head
    self.head = self.head.next
    if self.head is None:
      self.tail = self.head
    self.head = None
    del node
    return

  print("poll: " + str(self.tail.data))
  node = self.tail
  self.tail = self.tail.prev
  if self.tail is None:
    self.head = self.tail
  self.tail = None
  del node

solutions.python:
class Solution:
  def __init__(self):
    self.head = None
    self.tail = None

  def add(self, data):
    # Add the data to the tail of the queue
    node = Node(data)
    if self.head is None:
      self.head = node
      self.tail = node
    else:
      self.tail.next = node
      node.prev = self.tail
      self.tail = node

  def poll(self):
    if self.head is None:
      print("poll: queue is empty")
      return -1
    if self.head.next is None:
      data = self.head.data
      node = self.head
      self.head = self.head.next
      if self.head is None:
        self.tail = self.head
      self.head = None
      del node
      return data

    data = self.tail.data
    node = self.tail
    self.tail = self.tail.prev
    if self.tail is None:
      self.head = self.tail
    self.tail = None
    del node
    return data

if __name__ == '__main__':
  s = Solution()
  n = int(input())
  while n:
    s.add(int(input()))
    n -= 1
  while s.head:
    print(s.poll(), end=" ")
    s.poll()
  print()
